$comment: D-PP-PRESCAN-ANGLE-MACRO-SPLICE-AUTHORITATIVE-LIVENESS witness (the sqlite test_syscall.c `#if SQLITE_OS_UNIX` -> `#include <errno.h>` shape). A QUOTE-included platform.h defines DSS_USE_ERRNO 1; the C19-C21 pre-scan is blind to that quote-include define, so `#if DSS_USE_ERRNO` folds to 0 in the pre-scan while the authoritative pass reads it LIVE. Before the fix the shipped errno object-macro splice under the guard was skipped -> `error[S0001]: got errno` on valid code. The fix splices the shipped `#define` regardless of the pre-scan verdict (the authoritative pass, which elides dead-branch defines, arbitrates liveness). errno=EINVAL(22)+EACCES(13) read/written through the real (*__errno_location()) libc accessor -> exit 42. elf x86_64 + arm64 (the errno macro's elf variant is arch-invariant); macho arm64 uses (*__error()) with the same flat EINVAL/EACCES values. The release arm runs the optimizer over the macro-expanded deref/call. RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the gate change -> the pre-scan skips the splice -> errno undeclared -> S0001 -> compile fails.
